title: WRITEBACK-RAG: Training the Knowledge Base through Evidence Distillation and Write-Back Enrichment

headings.text: W RITE BACK -RAG Prevents Answer Leakage
W RITE BACK -RAG as a General Method for RAG
Rationale for using Naive RAG for Analysis

section_anchors.text: We argue that the knowledge base should be treated as a trainable component and propose W RITE BACK -RAG
Cross-method transfer experiments further show that the distilled knowledge benefits RAG pipelines other than the one used to produce it
This observation motivates a new concept we call knowledge base training
a two-stage gating mechanism analyzes retrieval behavior to identify which training samples benefit from retrieval
A RAG system consists of three components: a retriever R, a generator G, and a knowledge base K
W RITE BACK -RAG instantiates the KB training objective by learning from how a RAG system interacts with the corpus on labeled data
The utility gate operates at the sample level, selecting training examples where retrieved knowledge makes a genuine difference
The document gate operates at the document level within each utility-approved example
Given a training query qi and its gated evidence, an LLM-based distiller F synthesizes a single knowledge unit
The distilled knowledge units are collected into a separate write-back corpus
We evaluate on six benchmarks from the FlashRAG collection
We use E5-base-v2 as the retriever with K=5 documents
We organize the analysis around five research questions
Table 2 reports results for all 48 settings
The utility gate selects vastly different fractions of training data depending on the task
To further understand how the document gate selects useful evidence, we analyze the retrieval-rank distribution of retained documents
A key question is whether write-back knowledge is specific to the RAG method that produced it
Table 4 ablates three controls of the write-back pipeline on NQ
We proposed W RITE BACK -RAG, a framework that treats the knowledge base as a trainable component of RAG systems
W RITE BACK -RAG has several limitations
Because W RITE BACK -RAG writes distilled knowledge back into a retrievable corpus, errors or biases in the distillation process may persist
The implementation of W RITE BACK -RAG is a corpus augmentation and distillation pipeline, not gradient-based optimization over KB parameters
Although the distiller never receives the gold answer, the utility gate selects examples where retrieval produces a correct answer

section_anchors.text: Our implementation uses one shared retriever encoder for both the original corpus and the write-back corpus
Figure 5 visualizes the relationship between extracted source evidence length and distilled write-back knowledge length across six datasets
We use task-specific prompts for retrieval-based inference
We next present representative training instances that were rewritten and added to the write-back corpus
We mainly adopt naive RAG as the reference baseline during analysis because it offers the most controlled setting for identifying the source of improvement
